key_points: Dorms have been significantly upgraded since the 1990s
Mold and mustiness in dorms is a real concern students ask about
SEA housing exists as a specialized option for marine science majors
key_quotes: Lived in The Gardens with 4 room mates in 1995; we called it the 'ghetto'. They've obviously upgraded the heck out of it now.
Curious to know if the dorms are musty or moldy? Have you heard of CC having any mold issues in the dorms?
Did you do SEA housing as a marine science major or not?

key_points: Baseball culture is a dominant and celebrated part of campus identity
Party school reputation exists but isn't the whole picture
Myrtle Beach proximity makes CCU a destination school with a fun atmosphere
key_quotes: My completely unbiased opinion is that our baseball field is rivaled by Swayze Field at Ole Miss... and no one else.
I went to a decent amount of coastal games as a freshman at CCU
Such a fun field to play on

key_points: No meaningful student data available on dining quality or options
No complaints or praise surfaced in any source
Treat this as an unknown — visit and test the food yourself
key_quotes: No direct quotes found in the available data regarding dining.

key_points: No direct mentions of counseling or mental health resources in any source
Students openly admit struggling with motivation and GPA drops
Covid-era academic disengagement was a real pattern among current students
key_quotes: Freshman year and Sophomore year I had a 2.8 gpa because I was lazy and thought because covid I didn't have to give effort
I dug myself into a hole freshman year with a 2.3 by the end of the year due to me being lazy cause of Covid

key_points: International students pay roughly $9,000/year in tuition — relatively affordable
International students warned they are seen as 'easy money' by universities
No specific mentions of scholarships, aid packages, or financial support programs
key_quotes: I can either go to Coastal Carolina University, study CS for one year then transfer
Contact the schools if they have any international student-specific scholarships. Those are very rare since international students are seen as easy money for uni's.

key_points: Marine science and marine biology programs are a clear standout draw
CS program is ABET accredited, signaling baseline engineering credibility
Students frequently ask if business programs are worth it — no strong endorsement found
key_quotes: I got accepted for the marine bio program next year and this really helps
I'm dying to be in the marine science program
Do you believe the business programs are the best of their programs?

key_points: Admission counselors appear accessible and give direct, personalized guidance
Freshman live-on requirements exist but exceptions may be possible for locals
No major complaints about bureaucracy or administrative failures surfaced
key_quotes: The admission counselor I was talking to said as long as I take college algebra and pass I don't have to submit test scores.
As a freshman would I have to live in the dorms or do I have the option to live in my own house?

key_points: Campus is described as beautiful by multiple alumni and visitors
Located in Conway, directly adjacent to Myrtle Beach — a major lifestyle perk
Baseball facilities alone draw visitors and generate pride campus-wide
key_quotes: It's a beautiful facility, amazing campus!
I live here in Conway so I go to the camps every summer
I was here about a month ago for a random trip for my 17th birthday, instantly fell in love with the school!

key_points: No concrete data on job placement rates or post-grad outcomes in any source
Marine science program appears career-focused with dedicated housing tracks
CCU produced MLB player Tommy La Stella — athletics-to-pro pipeline exists
key_quotes: No idea La Stella went there.
Do you believe the average person could manage the school work while working a part or even full time job?

key_points: At ~$9,000/year for international students, tuition is notably competitive
Athletic investment is massive — $16M baseball facility at a mid-major school
Students use CCU as a strategic stepping stone for transfers, suggesting mixed long-term value
key_quotes: I can either go to Coastal Carolina University, study CS for one year then transfer to the school I want
Having your own weight room at a mid major school is unbelievable.
These facilities are on par with all the major programs in the country.

key_points: School has a passionate, sports-proud identity anchored by baseball and beach proximity
Strong pull for marine science students who see it as a dream fit
Academically, it attracts students who are rebuilding GPAs or seeking a relaxed environment
key_quotes: I just found out that I got in today. So now I'm really considering it.
I'm stressed and can't see myself doing anything else or going anywhere else
Ever since winning the Natty, things have totally changed including the stadium.
